Notes

VfM source notes

Start with the source, then map the concepts

This section collects value for money evaluation notes. Start with the definitions synthesis, then follow the source notes and map to see how citations and linked source material connect.

Source notes VfM criteria Evidence map Export BibTeX

Source Notes

Overview Synthesis

VfM Definitions

Compares authoritative definitions from HM Treasury, the National Audit Office, and the OECD DAC evaluation criteria.

Central Gov Guidance Source note

Green Book VfM Guidance

Summarises HM Treasury’s Green Book definition of value for money and maps appraisal concepts to VfI criteria, subcriteria, standards, and evidence prompts.

Central Gov Guidance Source note

Magenta Book VfM Evaluation

Summarises HM Treasury’s Magenta Book guidance on value for money evaluation, including appraisal links, VfM methods, Four Es, rubric-based approaches, and mixed evidence synthesis.

Central Gov Guidance Source note

Government Efficiency Framework Summary

Summarises HM Treasury’s concise GEF definitions and criteria for valid efficiency savings, including output protection, net savings, recurrence, and evidence quality.

Central Gov Guidance Source note

Government Efficiency Framework

Summarises HM Treasury’s full framework for defining, tracking, reporting, assuring, and governing central government efficiency savings.

Central Gov Guidance Synthesis

Government Efficiency Framework and VfI Rubrics

Maps GEF material to VfI-style criteria, subcriteria, performance-standard prompts, and evidence requirements without creating a full rubric.

Central Gov Guidance Source note

JSP 507 Investment Appraisal and Evaluation

Summarises MOD’s appraisal and evaluation guidance and maps it to VfI criteria, subcriteria, performance-standard prompts, and evidence planning for defence programmes.

Central Gov Guidance Source note

NAO VfM Definition

Summarises the National Audit Office definition of good value for money through economy, efficiency, effectiveness, and intended outcomes.

King and VfI Source note

OPM King VfM Guidance

Summarises OPM’s approach to assessing value for money, including criteria, rubrics, evidence standards, synthesis, judgement, and reporting.

King and VfI Source note

Verian Value for Investment Guidance

Summarises King and Hurrell’s Verian guide to evaluating value for money in UK public services, including the relationship between cost-benefit analysis and broader Value for Investment assessment.

OECD DAC Source note

OECD DAC Evaluation Criteria

Summarises the OECD DAC evaluation criteria, including relevance, coherence, effectiveness, efficiency, impact, sustainability, and the principles for thoughtful use.

OECD DAC Synthesis

VfI and OECD DAC Relationship

Explains how Julian King’s Value for Investment framing can use the OECD DAC criteria as evaluation lenses while adding standards, synthesis, and an explicit resource-use judgement.

OECD DAC Source note

Defence Logistics Vessel DAC VfM Evaluation

Summarises an ex-post evaluation of HNoMS Maud, showing how a DAC-inspired framework can assess operational, tactical, and strategic defence project success.

DfT Source note

DfT VfM Framework

Summarises DfT’s framework for transport value for money assessment, including BCRs, non-monetised impacts, risk, uncertainty, and VfM categories.

DfT Source note

DfT Transport Business Case

Summarises how DfT applies the five-case model, TAG, Green Book guidance, and value for money evidence in transport investment decisions.

DfT Source note

DfT TAG Appraisal and Evaluation

Summarises Transport Analysis Guidance as DfT’s technical base for transport modelling, appraisal, evaluation planning, and analytical assurance.

DfT Source note

DfT VfM Indicator

Summarises DfT’s portfolio indicator for expected value for money at approval and distinguishes it from evaluation of achieved value.

DfT Source note

DfT VfM Case Examples

Summarises DfT’s Integrated Transport Block and major bus-related scheme value for money assessments as practical portfolio examples.

Sourcing Playbook Synthesis

Sourcing and Consultancy Playbooks for VfM Rubric Creation

Sets transparent relevance rules for using sourcing and consultancy playbook material in VfM rubric creation.

Sourcing Playbook Source note

Sourcing Playbook

Summarises Cabinet Office guidance on service delivery, outsourcing, insourcing, mixed economy sourcing, and contracting.

Sourcing Playbook Source note

Sourcing Framework Agreements

Explains how Sourcing Playbook responsibilities apply when using framework agreements and call-off contracts.

Sourcing Playbook Source note

Sourcing Delivery Model Assessment

Summarises evidence-based assessment of in-house, outsourced, and hybrid delivery options.

Sourcing Playbook Source note

Sourcing Should Cost Modelling

Summarises should-cost modelling as whole-life cost evidence for sourcing and VfM judgement.

Sourcing Playbook Source note

Sourcing Bid Evaluation

Summarises how bid evaluation balances price, quality, social value, and deliverability.

Sourcing Playbook Source note

Sourcing Benefits Measurement

Summarises commercial benefits measurement, benefits registers, reviews, and benefits reporting.

Sourcing Playbook Source note

Sourcing Market Management

Summarises market health, supplier access, competition, and market stewardship as VfM evidence.

Sourcing Playbook Source note

Sourcing Testing and Piloting

Summarises testing and piloting as evidence for service viability, user needs, and staged commitment.

Sourcing Playbook Source note

Sourcing Risk Allocation and Pricing

Summarises risk allocation, pricing approaches, incentives, and payment mechanisms.

Sourcing Playbook Source note

Sourcing Supplier Financial Standing

Summarises assessment and monitoring of supplier financial capacity and financial risk.

Sourcing Playbook Source note

Consultancy Playbook

Summarises guidance on commissioning consultants, improving outcomes, and building Civil Service capability.

Sourcing Playbook Source note

Consultancy Knowledge and Skills Transfer

Summarises guidance on knowledge generation, skills transfer, sharing, and reuse in consultancy engagements.

Notes Map

Internal nodes are limited to the Notes section. External nodes show cited references and linked source pages.

internal Notes pages external Cited and linked sources Hover to highlight links, click for details and navigation, then drag to reposition or scroll to zoom.
